programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog and the posse travel to the Big Island to help Leland track down a small-time Bonnie and Clyde couple. Their crime: theft, including allegedly stealing a pair of Buddha statues for resale. But this only masks deeper problems with the couple.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog is in Denver helping Bobby Brown to track down a fugitive with a long rap sheet. But unfortunately they have no picture of the felon to work with, so the challenges are big on this one.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog goes after Virginia, a hard-to-find lawbreaker. He gets a tip that a pickup truck will be going to Virginia's house. In true private-eye fashion.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog and Beth take on the heartbreaking case of Rachel. Rachel's mom bailed her out of jail on condition that she go to AA meetings and stick to a curfew.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Kristine had a string of gambling and forgery charges before she jumped bail leaving Dog "holding the bag" -- in this case a $20,000 bond. Now it's time for Dog to call in some chits of his own.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog is back in Denver and in hot pursuit of a woman wanted for identity theft. The sad reality is that the identify which has been robbed is her own. She's traded in a bright future for a life on the wrong side of the law.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Today, Dog and the boys are going after a fugitive with a serious price tag. Tim failed to appear in court on a $25,000 bond and has a string of theft charges behind him.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog and the team go after Kandice, AKA Candy, AKA Suga, a stripper who didn't make her court appearance. They convince one of her friends to lure her to a supermarket parking lot, where Dog has an easy time fetching the fetching young thing.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Today Dog finds himself looking for more than one fugitive. He starts the day looking for a woman who has skipped out on her court date.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog is back in Hawaii and tracking a dangerous fugitive through the jungles of the Big Island. Their prey is being protected by a ring of family and friends that Dog must forcefully confront to bring in his man.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: The team is still in Colorado, and today Dog starts his day by to giving his kids and crew a sample of their western heritage by taking them horseback riding. This hunt takes Dog back to his old stomping grounds in Denver.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Being hunted by the Dog is a lousy way to celebrate your birthday but that's exactly the spot that Robert finds himself in today. He's missed his court date, quit his job, and vacated his last known address.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog has a big problem. Patty Estabilio has jumped a $15,000 bail bond and disappeared into Hawaii's underworld. Kissing the kids good night, Dog and Beth, with Tim and Leland at their side, plunge into a drug-infused netherworld.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: When Leland learns that one of his clients with a reputation for stealing has lost her co-signer and could be ready to run, Dog and the crew hop a plane to the Big Island.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: When a stripper goes on the lam, Dog has to bring her in--it's a tough job but somebody has to do it. Thankfully for the team, Lady Luck is presiding over the posse tonight.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog is a big-hearted guy and today he's proving it. He's helping Mary Ellen, his friend and fellow bondsman, on a dangerous night hunt for a fugitive she is now regretting bailing out.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: When Beth returns to the compound on the Big Island she finds a damaged SUV and two possible culprits--Dog or Justin. Pressed by Beth, Dog offers a third option. The perpetrators are Hawaii's mythical mischief makers--the Menehune!

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: It's been quite a while since Tim Chapman put down his mace can and bulletproof vest to care for his growing family. When his old friend Dog calls asking for help tracking an elusive felon Tim jumps into action.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog's latest quarry is running from abuse and assault charges. As Dog investigates, he discovers that this fugitive has gamed the system by jumping from one bond, and one bondsman, to another.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: What starts out as a perfect day for Dog turns ugly fast. He and Beth just bought bunnies for their kids, and Dog is enjoying helping them learn to care for their new pets. But duty calls and this is a tough case to fathom.

programme.desc: Being hunted by the Dog is a lousy way to celebrate your birthday but that's exactly the spot that Robert finds himself in today. He's missed his court date, quit his job, and vacated his last known address.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog has a big problem. Patty Estabilio has jumped a $15,000 bail bond and disappeared into Hawaii's underworld. Kissing the kids good night, Dog and Beth, with Tim and Leland at their side, plunge into a drug-infused netherworld.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: When Leland learns that one of his clients with a reputation for stealing has lost her co-signer and could be ready to run, Dog and the crew hop a plane to the Big Island.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: When a stripper goes on the lam, Dog has to bring her in--it's a tough job but somebody has to do it. Thankfully for the team, Lady Luck is presiding over the posse tonight.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog is a big-hearted guy and today he's proving it. He's helping Mary Ellen, his friend and fellow bondsman, on a dangerous night hunt for a fugitive she is now regretting bailing out.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: When Beth returns to the compound on the Big Island she finds a damaged SUV and two possible culprits--Dog or Justin. Pressed by Beth, Dog offers a third option. The perpetrators are Hawaii's mythical mischief makers--the Menehune!

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: It's been quite a while since Tim Chapman put down his mace can and bulletproof vest to care for his growing family. When his old friend Dog calls asking for help tracking an elusive felon Tim jumps into action.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog's latest quarry is running from abuse and assault charges. As Dog investigates, he discovers that this fugitive has gamed the system by jumping from one bond, and one bondsman, to another.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: What starts out as a perfect day for Dog turns ugly fast. He and Beth just bought bunnies for their kids, and Dog is enjoying helping them learn to care for their new pets. But duty calls and this is a tough case to fathom.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog and company head to the Big Island in pursuit of two courthouse no-shows only to find that one of them appears to be posting status updates from Southern California.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog goes after a fugitive named Tracy who lives in a homeless shelter, but the shelter's staff is uncooperative, and the two sides get into an angry confrontation that forces Dog to temporarily back off.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: When an offender with a long record ducks his day in court, Bobby Brown knows whom to call--The Dog! And knowing that few souls can resist Beth's clever persuasion, Dog gives her the job of luring the fugitive in.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog shows his love of justice and children in this poignant episode. He begins by giving encouragement to a group of handicapped children and making them honorary members of his posse.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: So what does a bondsman do when he learns that the felon he's bonded out is booked on an afternoon flight to Florida? Especially when over $140,000 is at stake? If you're Dog, you 911 the team and race to Honolulu International.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: No one becomes a bondsman overnight and even the members of Dog's team know there is always more to learn. So when Duane Lee cuts a felon a little too much slack about checking-in and then goes missing, Duane Lee knows he's gonna get schooled.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Baby Lyssa's wedding is only three days away, but Dog is thinking about bounties, not bouquets. A hot jump has comes in and Dog wants to go on the hunt.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Dog's target for today has been in trouble with the law since he was a juvenile. And he seems willing to use any trick in the system to avoid capture by Dog. In this match of wits, will Dog prevail?

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: When the local cops put out an APB for a client with a checked past, Dog springs into action. With the aid of the runner's girlfriend the posse heads out to bring him in.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: When the co-signer of a bond is also romantically involved with the defendant, sometimes the bondman is put in the middle of a lover's quarrel. But sometimes the defendant is a threat to the community and the cosigner asks the bondsman for protection.

programme.title: Dog the Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: When can a bounty hunt also be a date? When Dog and Beth decide to ride in the same SUV as they pursue a run away fugitive, will their different approaches to the investigation lead to a capture and romance?

programme.desc: Dog's target for today was once an upstanding member of his community. Now with no home, no job and the law on his trail, this middle-aged exile from middle-class society is using the cold end of an ice pipe to soothe his troubled soul.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Giorgio Tsoukalos and David Childress explore the mysterious chain of manmade islets in Micronesia's Nan Madol; built from 250 million tons of volcanic rock, there are local legends that strange visitors floated the massive rocks through the air.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Theorists suggest that some extraterrestrials may closely resemble animals, as evidenced by gods depicted with animal traits; researchers suggest that some animals may have been planted here by aliens as part of an experiment to shape life on Earth.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: "Ancient Aliens" is counting down the world's top 10 pyramid sites, from a valley in Peru with over 250 hidden pyramids to an ancient Greek city with mysterious 4,800-year-old ruins to Egypt's famous Giza Plateau.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Giorgio Tsoukalos takes a look back at the most compelling investigations of all, and events that he believes represent real encounters with extraterrestrial visitors.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Mysterious relics and sealed vaults may have been hidden on purpose and protected by an extraterrestrial source.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: In August of 2014, Russian cosmonauts aboard the International Space Station discover living sea plankton covering parts of the windows; there might be alien life forms that inhabit the seas.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: The ancient site of Gobekli Tepe challenges what archaeologists believe about the past; the megalithic stone complex was built over 12,000 years ago, 5,000 years before the first known civilization was said to have emerged in Mesopotamia.

programme.title: Killer Cases
programme.desc: Makeva Jenkins, a successful businesswoman and mother of three, boasted on Facebook that she had grown her business to six figures; hours later, she is shot dead with a bullet to the head.

programme.title: Killer Cases
programme.desc: In 1993, a newborn baby's body is discovered; the coroner determines the baby had been born alive; after 25 years, and with the assistance of modern DNA techniques, Gail Eastwood-Ritchey is found guilty of murder and sentenced to life in prison.

programme.title: Killer Cases
programme.desc: Todd Kendhammer calls 911 in a panic, describing a freak accident involving his wife; police are suspicious of Todd's responses in the interrogation room; forensic details from the autopsy report cast further doubt on his story.

programme.title: Killer Cases
programme.desc: On a summer night, Amanda Colley returns to a ransacked house; her love interest Lamar and two girlfriends arrive to join her, and gunshots ring out; Amanda and her friend Lindy are found dead.

programme.title: Killer Cases
programme.desc: When a hairstylist failed to pick up her children for a planned celebration, she was reported missing by her ex-husband; investigators discovered a bloody scene at the salon, but the hairstylist and her SUV were gone.

programme.title: Killer Cases
programme.desc: Ten days after NBA player Lorenzen Wright goes missing, the player's body is found in a swamp, shot 11 times; police search for clues to identify who killed the basketball star.

programme.desc: In Cree culture, a promise is sacred. When Cree woman Shirley Soosay goes missing in 1980, her niece Violet promises her grandmother that she will bring Shirley home.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A young mother and her infant son mysteriously disappeared from their home. The woman's husband was the only suspect, but he was eventually cleared. There was no solid evidence that a crime had even been committed. Six months later, a local farmer found the remains of the missing woman and her child. Both had been murdered. Agents and local police had only one tiny piece of evidence, an unidentified hair found in the murdered woman's car. The hair was too small for analysis.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the early 1980's, a pair of serial bank robbers gained a reputation among law enforcement officials as formidable bandits. Using the same modus operandi, the two men took hostages and emptied bank vaults across the Midwest. After the criminals escaped from police custody, FBI agents familiar with the bandits believed that they would attempt a grand-scale heist. By conducting surveillance on known associates of the criminals, authorities tried to anticipate the next target.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: This documentary series takes viewers behind the scenes of major crime investigations, as agents and scientists recount their forensic detective work.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On February 26, 1993, an explosion rocked the World Trade Center parking garage. Six people died and more than a thousand were injured in the bomb blast that tore a 5-story crater in the building. During the investigation, the twisted fender of a rental van was extracted from the rubble. The FBI was able to determine from where the van was rented and by whom. Undercover agents then made their arrests within six days of the bombing.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On a chilly afternoon in 1989, Marine Captain Shirley Russell disappeared from Quantico, Virginia. Captain Russell was a dedicated officer; her friends and colleagues were certain she had not gone AWOL. FBI agents found her husband, a former Marine, was acting strangely, and a blood-like stain on the couple's floor disappeared before agents could examine it. There was no body, no witness and no weapon.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: This documentary series takes viewers behind the scenes of major crime investigations, as agents and scientists recount their forensic detective work.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Georgia, a series of bizarre shootings terrorizes the town of East Point. Two people are killed, and two are critically wounded. At first the shootings seem targeted, then they seem random. As the body count climbs, the killer taunts law enforcement with mysterious notes. Agents believe the shooter will continue until he is deador behind bars.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the 1980s, the drug trade ravages Chicago's inner city neighborhoods and violent crime threatens to destroy entire communities. As drug profits grow, gangs rule the streets. One of the most dangerous is the El Rukns. FBI Agents team up with state and local police to bring them down, but in the process, uncover a bizarre and frightening terrorist plot.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the late 70s and early 80s, a terrorist group known as the FALN is suspected of setting off bombs in major U.S. cities. Their goal - to bring attention to the Puerto Rican independence movement. As the bombings escalate, innocent lives are lost. FBI agents and police are is in a deadly race to stop this murderous group with a dangerous cause.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the mid-1990's, a gang of professional bank robbers raided cities across seven states. Disguises and speed protected their identity from cameras and the police as they stole hundreds of thousands of dollars, always leaving a bomb to terrorize their victims. The stakes were raised when the FBI discovered the robbers had an agenda beyond their personal wealth. To stop these extremists and their growing operation, the FBI needed to capture their leaders.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1994, a rash of home invasion robberies take place in the metropolitan Detroit area. The thieves turn out to be part of a local gang dubbed the Home Invaders. Their crimes extend to insurance fraud, theft, and illegal lottery schemes. The Violent Crimes Task Force (VCTF) opens an investigation, and with the cooperation of a gang member who was apprehended at the scene of a crime, they are able to obtain the names, addresses, and telephone numbers of 16 gang members.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1997, FBI agents began investigating New York gas station mogul Gurmeet Dhinsa for gas pump rigging. They soon realized that Dhinsa was more than a crooked businessman, he enforced his mob-like operation with brutal violence, kidnapping and killing errant employees and any family members who questioned their deaths. FBI agents worked their way into Dhinsa's violent world.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the mid-1990's, a gang of professional bank robbers raided cities across seven states. Disguises and speed protected their identity from cameras and the police as they stole hundreds of thousands of dollars, always leaving a bomb to terrorize their victims. The stakes were raised when the FBI discovered the robbers had an agenda beyond their personal wealth. To stop these extremists and their growing operation, the FBI needed to capture their leaders.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: An Arkansas State Trooper is shot and killed after pulling over a van for a traffic violation. Officers apprehend the shooter, after a car chase and shootout. ATF is alerted when officers find weapons and explosives in the man's vehicle. Further testing of the weapons reveals one was used in a murder several months earlier. ATF traces some of the firearms to purchases made by another man from and arms dealer. They also match silencer marks from the weapons to a third man previously arrested for stealing a flatbed truck.

programme.desc: An old flame of Nick's shows up. Although she's the one who cost him his job with the DEA, Nick is willing to let bygones be bygones, until he realizes that suspicious deaths seem to have followed her to Key Mariah.

programme.title: Tropical Heat
programme.desc: Nick Slaughter, a disgraced former Canadian Mountie, and DEA Agent moves to the tropical island paradise of Key Mariah. Once there he starts his own Private Detective Agency, and meets Sylvie Girard, a local travel agent. Together they form a strong friendship and quite the detective team. However, the local police squad never make it easy for them luckily they have plenty of friends to help along the way. The Town of Key Mariah will never be the same again thanks to Nick Slaughter and company!

programme.title: Tropical Heat
programme.desc: A woman hires Nick and Sylvie to find her mother who abandoned her 20 years ago. They track her down to a clinic that offers psychiatric treatment, where Nick and Sylvie pose as a couple to join the therapy group.

programme.title: Xploration Animal Science
programme.desc: Today we are hopping down to South America to check out some of the coolest creatures on Earth. Animals featured include the parrot, the sea lion, the tapir, the iguana, and more!

programme.title: Xploration Animal Science
programme.desc: Did you know a beavers teeth never stop growing and that it can use them to build dams big enough to see from outer space? A flock of Canada geese can migrate 1500 miles in a single day. Plus, the Bison can move 40 mph!

programme.title: Xploration Awesome Planet
programme.desc: Host Philippe Cousteau looks at ways scientists are measuring the health of our forests. In New England, Philippe visits a white pine forest that is being threatened by invasive plants. At the University of California - Santa Cruz, students use sonic-tomography to look inside trees in order to detect any signs of disease. At the Pine Barrens of New Jersey, we learn about the pitch pine tree.

programme.title: Xploration Awesome Planet
programme.desc: Host Philippe Cousteau looks at new ways of cultivating fresh, nutritious food. He visits a farm in Los Angeles where they grow food on a parking lot, just to show that fresh food can be grown in an urban environment. We also look into what distinguishes fruits from vegetables. The results can be surprising. And then we look at the amount of food that is wasted world wide.

programme.title: Xploration Awesome Planet
programme.desc: We head to a winter wonderland in Banff, Alberta, Canada. Philippe joins the avalanche safety team at Ski Lake Louise as they show a few different ways of avalanche control including dynamite blasts on the mountain. We then head to the Fairmont Chateau Lake Louise for a snowshoeing expedition. And finally we look at the dynamics of ice while climbing a frozen waterfall.

programme.title: Xploration Awesome Planet
programme.desc: We look at different examples of cultural science. Starting off in Hawaii, Philippe paddles out on a 6-man canoe during a lesson in early Hawaiian navigation. We wrap things up in the Pacific Northwest looking at the Suquamish tribe.

programme.title: Señora del Destino
programme.desc: María do Carmo es una madre de cinco hijos que triunfó en la vida con mucha lucha y tesón, pero que todavía tendrá una batalla que luchar: obtener el cariño de su hija quien fue secuestrada cuando era una recién nacida.

programme.title: Melissa
programme.desc: Defne tiene una pesadilla con el señor Fezvi y su padre. Metin le confiesa a su hermana que le sucedió algo extraño en casa de Öyku. Defne escapa por la noche y corre a casa de Melissa. Orhan visita la casa de Cüneyt.

programme.title: Destinos cruzados
programme.desc: Toprak despierta de un coma tras cinco años sin recordar nada de su pasado, y regresa a una familia que no reconoce, incluida su prometida de toda la vida y un niño que vive con él. Sin embargo, su amnesia lo mantiene en una incertidumbre constante.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Said es un gran empresario adinerado, la inversión que va a hacer es con la familia Ferrer. Lucas se aleja de esas negociaciones, pero pide que le averigüen si Said vino con su esposa.

programme.title: Eres mi estrella
programme.desc: La compañía que maneja el grupo ídolo ANJELL insiste en contratar un nuevo cantante porque la voz del cantante principal Tae Kyung, ya no es la misma.

programme.title: Génesis
programme.desc: Adaptación de uno de los libros más reconocidos de la Biblia, que aborda la historia del origen de la humanidad, invitando a entender el presente, volviendo al principio de todo. La caída de Lucifer, la vida de Adán y Eva, Noé y el diluvio, y otros.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle

programme.title: Always Remember Your Name
programme.desc: A documentary of mistaken identity, hope, love, and the power of story by two Holocaust survivors.

programme.title: WCVB 50 Years Special
programme.desc: (2022) WCVB Channel 5 celebrates 50 years - from the very first moments on the air to meeting the people who make it all happen.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Many of the towns of Boston’s North Shore are thought of simply as bedroom communities. However, they are more than just homes for people who work in the Hub. Beverly, Salem, Ipswich and Swampscott have rich maritime histories and traditions still alive today. Tonight, Ted Reinstein travels the Main Streets and Back Roads around Route 1A North.

programme.title: The Best of Chronicle: Main Streets and Back Roads
programme.desc: It’s not New Hampshire’s oldest or longest highway, but it is one of its most storied. Tonight, Ted Reinstein makes a return trip to Route 4, including the stretch known as “Antique Alley” where he finds vintage records (and a very large stuffed shark). Ted’s trip continues east where he paddles through a salt marsh found, improbably, 20 miles inland from the Seacoast. He fuels up at the classic Gilley’s Diner in Portsmouth before touring the historic and moving sites along Portsmouth’s Black Heritage Trail.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Since the Earl of Sandwich accidentally created them, it seems New Englanders have been working to perfect the art of the sandwich. Tonight we embark on a mouthwatering sandwich safari

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Cheese makers, educators, artisans – tonight we meet them all! Erika Tarantal goes elbow-deep to make mozzarella during a visit to Narragansett Creamery, and we meet the goats at Valley View Farm whose milk produces chevre, feta, and camembert. Charcuterie boards are all the rage and Shayna Seymour learns the true art of assembly at Boards by Mo. Hungry yet? We hit up Civility Social House for a mac-and-cheese donut (!), and the Say Cheese grilled cheese truck. And did you know you can study cheese in college? We’ll tell you where.

programme.title: FIXT
programme.desc: Ted wants to live to 100 with his beloved wife beside him. But his oral health threatens his future. Kristy’s a FIXT staffer desperate to smile big on her wedding day. Will love help them face their fears?

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: If it kills you to throw anything away, find inspiration in our stories tonight. We trace what happens after people leave unwanted clothing in a collection bin, and meet an entrepreneur who turns old skis and snowboards into seating. At The Great Exchange in Devens, nonprofits shop for office furniture and other leftovers donated by area businesses. And secondhand stores aren’t just for clothing and furniture: Make & Mend offers gently used arts and crafts supplies.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: The world is watching as millions of displaced Ukrainians struggle to find safety. If you were suddenly one of the 26 million refugees around the world, what would you do? Tonight Nicole Estaphan shares stories of refugees from Ukraine, Afghanistan, and Syria, who are beginning new lives in Massachusetts and beyond. In Methuen, we meet an Afghan journalist starting from scratch with his wife and young daughter. Nicole visits a Newburyport church that houses refugee families, and talks with the Boston-based non-profit Beyond Conflict. Finally, Erika Tarantal brings us the story of one refugee who made it from war-torn Bosnia to Brookline, where she now owns a small business.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: We’ve all heard of the Coney Island hot dog eating contest, but Shayna Seymour and Erika Tarantal are curious: What about food competitions right here in New England?

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Martha’s Vineyard is Shayna’s “happy place.” She spends every summer on the island, but this year, she had a chance to visit during springtime. Back on the mainland, she met a young man who turned his love of comic books into a business. Also this year, she had a chance to “pixel” herself (you have to watch to understand). Tonight, a look back at Shayna’s favorite stories of 2021.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: The Best of Chronicle: Main Streets and Back Roads
programme.desc: Nestled in a bend of the Deerfield River with its famous Bridge of Flowers, classic Main Street, and dramatic waterfall, Shelburne Falls is right out of a movie set. It is no wonder *three* major productions have come to town in recent years. As Anthony Everett points out, Shelburne Falls is the scene for movie make-believe because, technically, Shelburne Falls is not a real town at all. Tonight, the wonder and beauty of this slice of Western Massachusetts.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: In Shelburne we find local charm, fascinating history, a classic general store, and a bevy of (teddy) bears – all wrapped in a blanket of gorgeous fall color. The 1,400-acre Shelburne Farms is famed for its Vermont cheddar – and gorgeous grounds laid out by Frederick Law Olmsted. The Shelburne Museum is jam-packed with art and oddities. We meet a well-known musician whose side hustle is highlighting “weirdness.” And no trip to Shelburne is complete without a visit to the Vermont Teddy Bear Company.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight we take a walk in the shoes of New England shoe designers, collectors, and makers. We also discover how a bar mitzvah project grew into a national movement, Gotta Have Sole, to provide new sneakers for kids experiencing homelessness.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Just 10 shopping days left before Christmas! Tonight we bring you stories of local makers and entrepreneurs who are meeting customers where they live. We meet a couple crafting unique glass products on the North Shore, and discover a gift box service that specializes in local products. On Cape Cod, Nicole Estaphan visits the Christmas shop at Cape Abilities for gifts that give back – local products that benefit the nonprofit’s clients. Finally, you might expect to find only major national chains at the Burlington Mall – but we talk to local mom-and-pop vendors getting their mall moment, too.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: It’s gift-giving season, and tonight we bring you stories of local makers and entrepreneurs. We visit a glittering, nature-inspired shop in Lynn, owned by a husband-and-wife team.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: It’s gift-giving season, and we’re talking to local shop owners and entrepreneurs about the value in shopping local.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight, we’re off to ski towns, some just a few gallons of gas away. First, Anthony Everett heads to the Nashoba Valley Ski Area and explores the town of Westford (including Winnie the Pooh’s “house”) and an indoor butterfly garden; Shayna Seymour goes tubing at Butternut Ski Area in Great Barrington, then stops for some French pastries and checks in to at local bed and breakfast; and Erika Tarantal heads to Shrewsbury where she hits the slopes (and is put to work) at Ski Ward, grabs a bite to eat at a local sushi shop, and fuels up with some sugar at a local candy shop.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines the huge eyes of the dark-dwelling owl and giant squid.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Learn from the experts on what do with ‘old stuff,’ when to keep, gift or throw away.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: Best of Chronicle: New England Eats
programme.desc: In honor of National Dessert Day we’re diving into the Chronicle vault for our sugar fix! Are you a cake person or a pie person? Tonight you don’t have to choose as we revisit a specialty cake shop in Brookline and a pie shop in Somerville. We sample classic Italian pastries in Lawrence, and do a deep dive on everyone’s favorite sweet – chocolate.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: As winter in New England drags on we can use a little sparkle. Tonight, all things shiny: From the literal sparkle of gold at the Harvard Mineralogical and Geological Museum, to a night out in Cambridge with The Glitter Boys, who spin vinyl from the ‘70s and ‘80s.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: WCVB Local News
programme.desc: Get the latest news and weather from WCVB.

programme.title: Fighting for Freedom: The United States v. Hamid Hayat
programme.desc: 14 years after he was arrested on federal terrorism charges, a Northern California man is freed after his case is overturned.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Nails and knives…bears and ballet slippers. Tonight we meet people who have carved out “a particular set of skills.” How does one get into the knife-making business? A former chef fills us in. We meet local women bringing high-fashion to clients’ fingertips through extravagant nail art, and one whose special skill is fitting ballet dancers with their pointe shoes. Finally, a seamstress brings customers to tears with handmade teddy bears dedicated to lost loved ones.

programme.title: FIXT
programme.desc: Ted wants to live to 100 with his beloved wife beside him. But his oral health threatens his future. Kristy’s a FIXT staffer desperate to smile big on her wedding day. Will love help them face their fears?

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Dining out, dressing up, or planning a wedding, you can go high-end OR wallet-friendly. Tonight, we do both. We sample the innovative fare at high-end JiangNan Boston…then check out the daily specials at a local spot, Mandarin Cuisine.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: As the natural world blooms around us, we dig into our archive to showcase unforgettable springtime stories captured by longtime Chronicle videographer/producer Art Donahue. We meet experts in birdwatching and birdsong, and follow a still photographer who captures early spring beauty – just steps from the Mass Pike. Apple blossoms are a sight to behold at Brookfield Orchards in North Brookfield, while a family farm is an unlikely Shangri-La in Franklin. We also take a trip down south - driving through Virginia’s Shenandoah Valley.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Clean, categorize, purge… scrub, sort, and sage! Tonight we meet people who were built for spring cleaning.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Scanned the real estate listings lately? Inventory is low, prices are sky-high, and some buyers are going to extreme lengths to lock in a purchase. Tonight Shayna Seymour surveys the market, touring homes at the median price in Greater Boston (hint: it’s high!). We meet a Worcester mom who was forced to widen her search to find an affordable home, and families choosing to remodel rather than compete with other buyers. A local home inspector shares a few hints (and horror stories). Finally, if you’re just dreaming, Shayna gets a peek inside a $7 million mansion that offers a taste of the celebrity lifestyle.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Anthony Everett makes a winter visit to the serene lake and surrounding communities that helped inspire the Oscar-winning film, On Golden Pond. Tonight Anthony chats with the film’s screenwriter, who still calls this region home, and meets a couple who left sunny San Diego to run a Holderness bed-and-breakfast.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Sick of the snow? We have the perfect solution. Tonight, join Anthony Everett as he heads south to explore the clear waters and stunning beaches on St. John in the U.S. Virgin Islands.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Shayna Seymour ventures to Stonington, Connecticut, voted one of the prettiest coastal towns in New England – taking in the salt air on a harbor cruise and hitting a local winery. Ted Reinstein takes a drive to East Greenwich, Rhode Island, where his adventures take him from the town’s historic Main Street to a sailing adventure on Greenwich Bay. And Anthony Everett heads north, visiting South Hero, Vermont, where he checks out a dairy farm that has added wine to its repertoire and bikes to an eatery popular with local foodies.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Ted Reinstein meets local growers who are big on blooms. Their peonies and poppies, sweet peas and snapdragons, lift the mood and beautify the landscape. Among the growers: a physician with a side gig as a lavender farmer, a prominent architect who invites Ted into his secret garden on Beacon Hill, and a small-scale flower grower committed to sustainable practices.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: We’re back with another round of stories suggested by you – our Chronicle viewers.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: You call us, you email us, you load up our social media pages. So tonight we’re making YOU our associate producers with a collection of stories suggested by viewers.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: From the Boston Tea Party – where tea became a symbol of defiance and independence – to elegant afternoon teas. Tonight we are steeped in tea stories!

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: We’re back with another round of stories suggested by you, our viewers!

programme.title: The Best of Chronicle: Main Streets and Back Roads
programme.desc: Shayna Seymour heads north to the charming town of Stowe, nestled at the foot of Mount Mansfield, where she explores bucolic farms, a network of mountain biking trails, and shares a brew on the Vermont Beer Trail (with a member of the Von Trapp family). Shayna meets a local family that has transformed a former ski dorm into a cafe and antiques shop. At Percy Farm, a farming family is dedicated to rebuilding after a devastating fire. And is there a better way to spend a few hours than frolicking with golden retrievers? In Stowe, you can do just that.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: Eat Play Stay
programme.desc: Catch the perfect sunset after learning to sail or riding the rails. Devour a cheeseboard al fresco, sip on a cool drink poolside, or slide into a booth at a classic Italian restaurant. This is Newport.

programme.title: Sports Town Stories
programme.desc: Whether it's a wrestling star training through grief, champion rollerskaters challenged on the world stage, or a cycling team pushing through obstacles, Iowans don't back down when the going gets tough.

programme.title: Blind Kitchen
programme.desc: In this battle, it's sink or swim. Chef Josiah of Purple Ocean Superfood Bar in Waterford Lakes takes on Chef Daniel of The Moderne in the Mills 50 district. We’ve got a seafood extravaganza on this episode of Blind Kitchen.

programme.title: Completed Episodes
programme.desc: In what has to be the stinkiest episode we've ever had, Frank Nicotero asks Cincinnati locals wild and wacky news trivia. You'll be glad you can't smell some of these stories.

programme.desc: Greenwood was a thriving Black district in Tulsa when, 100 years ago, it was destroyed in less than 24 hours.

programme.desc: A celebration of the oldest, largest, and longest recreational touring bicycle ride in the world. Three riders and a pair of community leaders reach new personal heights, changing their lives along the seven day ride across Iowa.

programme.title: Boston Rob Does Beantown
programme.desc: A car or the T might be the typical way to travel, but Rob’s on a mission to explore some of the more unique modes of transport the Boston area’s got to offer.

programme.title: Rickwood
programme.desc: This is the story of the oldest professional ballpark in the U.S. and the determination of its home team, the Black Barons. The voice of Rickwood tells the tale of its pivotal role in The Negro League, Black history and its legacy in American sports.

programme.title: Weathering the Storm

programme.title: One Pill Can Kill
programme.desc: Told through the voices of grieving parents, this documentary follows the real and lasting impact of the fentanyl crisis. Capturing the aftermath of sudden loss, the search for answers, and the transformation of grief into public advocacy.

programme.title: Eat Play Stay
programme.desc: Take the high road for fabulous views of the Ozarks. Then take the low road deep into a cave as you pass underground waterfalls. Eat the best cheeseburgers around, introduce yourself to the village nut, and get crazy in a treehouse bar.

programme.title: Maker Nation Challenge
programme.desc: Costumes are a part of the fabric that makes up New Orleans. As three local designers will find out – nothing is easy in the Big Easy as they compete in creating one-of-a-kind costumes inspired by the Crescent City and the river that shapes it.

programme.title: Shift: The Ragbrai Documentary
programme.desc: A celebration of the oldest, largest, and longest recreational touring bicycle ride in the world. Three riders and a pair of community leaders reach new personal heights, changing their lives along the seven day ride across Iowa.

programme.title: Game Gone Wild: The Socker Slam Story
programme.desc: A stranger than fiction documentary that chronicles a semi-scripted extreme sporting league that premiered in 2000. Socker Slam took elements of wrestling, rugby and “butts up” shenanigans and body slammed them into a soccer drama.

programme.title: Americas Most Delish
programme.desc: Uncover the Midwest's unexpected. From quirky to inventive flavor mash-ups like the bulgogi burger to the questionable sour cream & raisin pie, see how the area crafts some interesting but delightful culinary concoctions.

programme.title: Stitch
programme.desc: Every day, around the country, people with disabilities find opportunity, overcome the odds and fight for their rights. These stories show how members of the disabled community are not to be overlooked or underestimated.

programme.title: My Perfect Day
programme.desc: Chris has an unstoppable spirit, despite the challenges of Down syndrome. His resilience has propelled him to extraordinary achievements, including clinching the gold in Greece. Meaghan takes him on a whirlwind of experiences tailored just for him.

programme.title: Ed & Day in The 'Burgh
programme.desc: Ed and Day travel to Homewood to see if they have what it takes to be urban beekeepers, elite barbers, and African dancers and drummers.

programme.title: Blind Kitchen
programme.desc: Puerto Rican Chefs Tanya Nicole of Lowell and Chef Calixto David from Chelsea spice it up in this Caribbean cook-off. Can Cali recreate a traditional dish flawlessly? Or will Tanya taste victory with a dish she’s never cooked before?

programme.title: Searching for Johnny
